message:
chore(deps): rename toon_rust dep to package tru + refresh fastembed / ort to unblock `cargo update`

Fixes the pre-existing resolver failure (flagged in 9fe58ce) where
`cargo update` couldn't advance the lockfile. Two coupled issues,
both unblocked here:

1. toon_rust git dep had no real crate to resolve against. The git
   repo at github.com/Dicklesworthstone/toon_rust publishes its crate
   as `tru` (the TOON reference implementation — binary name `tru`
   to avoid the coreutils `tr` collision), not `toon_rust`. The
   `toon_rust = { git = "…" }` line therefore always failed with
   "no matching package named `toon_rust`". Switch to the crates.io
   source with an explicit package rename so the src/ call sites
   (`toon_rust::encode(…)` on 7 lines in src/main.rs) keep working
   unchanged:
     - Cargo.toml line 55: `toon_rust = { git = "…/toon_rust" }`
       → `toon_rust = { version = "0.2", package = "tru" }`.

2. ort + fastembed version wall. frankensearch-embed (the git dep at
   HEAD, which carries the asupersync 0.3.0 bump) requires
   `ort = "=2.0.0-rc.11"`, but xf's own root pin was `ort =
   "2.0.0-rc.9"`, and the transitive path through
   `fastembed 4.9 → ort =2.0.0-rc.9` pinned us down. fastembed 5.x
   (current crates.io latest 5.13.x) moved to ort rc.11+, so bumping
   there unlocks the whole graph:
     - Cargo.toml line 81: `ort = { version = "2.0.0-rc.9", … }` →
       `ort = { version = "2.0.0-rc.11", … }`.
     - Cargo.toml line 80: `fastembed = { version = "4.9", … }` →
       `fastembed = { version = "5", … }`.

- Cargo.lock: full `cargo update` now succeeds. The lockfile collapses
  to a single asupersync 0.3.0 entry (previously was blocked on the
  toon_rust resolution failure). frankensearch-* pulls through its
  git HEAD at f6dc73aa, which is the commit I pushed earlier in this
  release wave that switched frankensearch's own asupersync dep from
  the old git rev 08dd31df to crates.io 0.3.0.

Source-code impact: none — the toon_rust alias preserves the import
surface, and ort/fastembed are used through frankensearch-embed's
re-exports at the xf callsites.
